cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The question is asking how many Palestinian prisoners had been released by Wednesday. The options are A) 21, B) 26, C) Over 26, D) 104. First, I need to read through the article carefully. The article says that Israel freed 26 Palestinian prisoners early Wednesday. It mentions that this is the second of four groups to be released. So, the first group was released before Wednesday, and the third and fourth will come later. The total number of prisoners to be released in all is 104, over four rounds. But the question specifically asks how many had been released by Wednesday. Since the second group was released on Wednesday, that's the 26 mentioned, plus the first group which was released earlier. Wait, the article says "the second of four groups to be released as part of an agreement that started the current Israeli-Palestinian peace talks." So if the second group was released on Wednesday, then the first group must have been released before Wednesday. So the total by Wednesday would be the first group plus the second group (26). However, the numbers of the first group aren't given in the article. The article only states that 26 were freed on Wednesday as the second group. Wait, the first sentence says Israel freed 26 Palestinian prisoners
